title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Mastery Check
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a plan-execute-verify task contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
Decompose work around independently verifiable outcomes and dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Reasoning Selection · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Use a changed quiz case for prompt reasoning planning that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Which action demonstrates Reasoning Selection without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: Use a changed quiz case for prompt reasoning planning that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Reasoning Selection condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.
